name: Emily Randall for Congress
subject: Everything you need to know about our adopted swing district
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress
body:
We wanted to give you an overview of why this race is so important – and how our combined efforts can turn this district from red to blue.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111921090/558827321/2145945666?refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.22_AZ06DeepDive_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNjdmYzlhZS0wYzY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VpMHPrN0y5CcoF5xod7lbEBBfUyUY5LE-tw-Y8WL4YU=&emci=5c6da55d-4866-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=167fc9ae-0c67-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) | Peter, **Earlier this year, Emily “adopted” one of the most competitive districts in the country, Arizona’s 6th, also known as AZ-06. ** That means we’re directing our grassroots energy to flip this critical seat ahead of the 2026 elections. We wanted to give you an overview of why this race is so important – and how our combined efforts can turn this district from red to blue.
_**The District**_ AZ-06 encompasses the southeastern corner of Arizona, including all of Greenlee County, and parts of Cochise, Pima, Pinal, and Graham Counties. Most of the District’s population lives in Pima County in the rapidly growing suburbs of Tucson. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111921091/558827323/755184972?refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.22_AZ06DeepDive_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNjdmYzlhZS0wYzY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VpMHPrN0y5CcoF5xod7lbEBBfUyUY5LE-tw-Y8WL4YU=&emci=5c6da55d-4866-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=167fc9ae-0c67-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111921092/558827324/193919884?refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.22_AZ06DeepDive_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNjdmYzlhZS0wYzY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VpMHPrN0y5CcoF5xod7lbEBBfUyUY5LE-tw-Y8WL4YU=&emci=5c6da55d-4866-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=167fc9ae-0c67-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) AZ-06 is truly a PURPLE district. Voters here backed Trump by 5% in 2016, then Biden by 0.1% (!!) in 2020, then Trump by 2% in 2024. However, the 6th District has supported Democratic candidates for Senate in 2020, 2022, and 2024.
_**The Incumbent**_ Republican **Juan Ciscomani** won this seat in 2022 by 1.5% and in 2024 by 2.5%. Despite representing an extremely purple district, he has voted lockstep with the MAGA agenda. When the Big Bad Bill was moving through Congress, Ciscomani [spoke out](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111921094/558827327/-1460501170?refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.22_AZ06DeepDive_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNjdmYzlhZS0wYzY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VpMHPrN0y5CcoF5xod7lbEBBfUyUY5LE-tw-Y8WL4YU=&emci=5c6da55d-4866-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=167fc9ae-0c67-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) against the provisions in the bill that would hurt Arizona’s green energy businesses. [He also said](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111921096/558827328/-1111014941?refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.22_AZ06DeepDive_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNjdmYzlhZS0wYzY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VpMHPrN0y5CcoF5xod7lbEBBfUyUY5LE-tw-Y8WL4YU=&emci=5c6da55d-4866-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=167fc9ae-0c67-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) he "cannot, and will not, support any legislation that reduces Medicaid benefits for vulnerable populations the program intended to serve." However, once Trump and Republican leaders applied a little pressure, Ciscomani caved and voted for the destructive budget he knew would hurt his constituents. The final bill contained a tax provision that [benefits Ciscomani personally](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111921098/558827329/-998883073?refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.22_AZ06DeepDive_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNjdmYzlhZS0wYzY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VpMHPrN0y5CcoF5xod7lbEBBfUyUY5LE-tw-Y8WL4YU=&emci=5c6da55d-4866-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=167fc9ae-0c67-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442), increasing the amount of “pass-through” income he can deduct from his taxes.
_**Our Candidate**_ While Ciscomani serves himself, our candidate **JoAnna Mendoza** has made a lifelong commitment to serving others. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111921099/558827330/-803653937?v=MWF6cmJ1OiI&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.22_AZ06DeepDive_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNjdmYzlhZS0wYzY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VpMHPrN0y5CcoF5xod7lbEBBfUyUY5LE-tw-Y8WL4YU=&emci=5c6da55d-4866-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=167fc9ae-0c67-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) JoAnna — known to friends as “Jo” — grew up in a farmworker family and relied on programs like SNAP and WIC. She enlisted in the United States Marine Corps at age 17 and served for 20 years – becoming a drill sergeant, deploying to Iraq and Afghanistan, and leading the next generation of Marines. She returned to Arizona to raise her son, Aidan, and worked as a nonprofit leader, veterans advocate, and policy expert to benefit working people in her community. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111921101/558827333/-803653937?v=MWF6cmJ1OiI&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.22_AZ06DeepDive_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNjdmYzlhZS0wYzY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VpMHPrN0y5CcoF5xod7lbEBBfUyUY5LE-tw-Y8WL4YU=&emci=5c6da55d-4866-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=167fc9ae-0c67-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) Saying that she would be an upgrade for Arizona’s 6th District is an understatement!
_**What’s Next?**_ There’s no handbook for “adopting” a swing district – but here are a few things we can do now: ➡️**Help with fundraising.** The earlier the Mendoza campaign brings in funds, the more effective they can be. ➡️ **Combat disinformation.** The GOP and their billionaire donors will target Jo. We can pitch in to counter any attacks that come her way. ➡️ **Grow our volunteer team.** Just like we pulled out all the stops to send Emily to Congress, we’ll do the same for Jo, with phonebanking and more. Now is the part where we ask for a donation. Most members of Congress don’t go all-in, over a year in advance, to help a campaign hundreds of miles away. But there aren’t normal times, so we’re trying something new. We need to flip three seats to take back the House, and AZ-06 could be one of them. [_**Please make a donation of $6, $26, or any amount to fuel our political program in Washington and in critical swing districts like Arizona’s 6th. Chip in today →**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111921105/558827336/1134054401?amount=6&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.22_AZ06DeepDive_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNjdmYzlhZS0wYzY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VpMHPrN0y5CcoF5xod7lbEBBfUyUY5LE-tw-Y8WL4YU=) [CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111921106/558827338/1134054401?amount=6&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.22_AZ06DeepDive_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNjdmYzlhZS0wYzY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VpMHPrN0y5CcoF5xod7lbEBBfUyUY5LE-tw-Y8WL4YU=) Thank you for reading this longer-than-usual email and for being part of this team 💙 – The whole team at Emily Randall for Congress

clean_body: We wanted to give you an overview of why this race is so important – and how our combined efforts can turn this district from red to blue. | Peter, Earlier this year, Emily “adopted” one of the most competitive districts in the country, Arizona’s 6th, also known as AZ-06. That means we’re directing our grassroots energy to flip this critical seat ahead of the 2026 elections. We wanted to give you an overview of why this race is so important – and how our combined efforts can turn this district from red to blue. The District AZ-06 encompasses the southeastern corner of Arizona, including all of Greenlee County, and parts of Cochise, Pima, Pinal, and Graham Counties. Most of the District’s population lives in Pima County in the rapidly growing suburbs of Tucson. AZ-06 is truly a PURPLE district. Voters here backed Trump by 5% in 2016, then Biden by 0.1% (!!) in 2020, then Trump by 2% in 2024. However, the 6th District has supported Democratic candidates for Senate in 2020, 2022, and 2024. The Incumbent Republican Juan Ciscomani won this seat in 2022 by 1.5% and in 2024 by 2.5%. Despite representing an extremely purple district, he has voted lockstep with the MAGA agenda. When the Big Bad Bill was moving through Congress, Ciscomani against the provisions in the bill that would hurt Arizona’s green energy businesses. he "cannot, and will not, support any legislation that reduces Medicaid benefits for vulnerable populations the program intended to serve." However, once Trump and Republican leaders applied a little pressure, Ciscomani caved and voted for the destructive budget he knew would hurt his constituents. The final bill contained a tax provision that , increasing the amount of “pass-through” income he can deduct from his taxes. Our Candidate While Ciscomani serves himself, our candidate JoAnna Mendoza has made a lifelong commitment to serving others. JoAnna — known to friends as “Jo” — grew up in a farmworker family and relied on programs like SNAP and WIC. She enlisted in the United States Marine Corps at age 17 and served for 20 years – becoming a drill sergeant, deploying to Iraq and Afghanistan, and leading the next generation of Marines. She returned to Arizona to raise her son, Aidan, and worked as a nonprofit leader, veterans advocate, and policy expert to benefit working people in her community. Just like we pulled out all the stops to send Emily to Congress, we’ll do the same for Jo, with phonebanking and more. Now is the part where we ask for a donation. Most members of Congress don’t go all-in, over a year in advance, to help a campaign hundreds of miles away. But there aren’t normal times, so we’re trying something new. name: Jonathan Nez
subject: My campaign is kicking off
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
My name is Jonathan Nez and today I’m officially launching my campaign to become the next Congressman for Arizona’s 2nd Congressional District – one of the most critical districts to win to take back the House.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250722-1?refcode=em20250722-2203&refcode2=2203_21716_8eQNia&akid=2203%2E21716%2E8eQNia)
Hi there,
My name is Jonathan Nez and today I’m officially launching my campaign to become the next Congressman for Arizona’s 2nd Congressional District – one of the most critical districts to win to take back the House.
As a man driven by faith, family, and love, my campaign is about bringing people from all walks of life together to create meaningful change for our district.
In 2024, our campaign delivered one of the strongest overperformances in the nation. We had over 78,000 grassroots online donors, and put AZ-2 on the map to the point that this is now one of the DCCC’s “Districts in Play” – meaning that winning this seat is critical to taking back the House.
[**Before I say more, I want you to know: the early days of a campaign can make or break it. Will you help me start strong by splitting a donation between my campaign and Sarah McBride to close our $104,319 budget gap?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250722-1?refcode=em20250722-2203&refcode2=2203_21716_8eQNia&akid=2203%2E21716%2E8eQNia)
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250722-1?refcode=em20250722-2203&refcode2=2203_21716_8eQNia&akid=2203%2E21716%2E8eQNia)**
Following my graduation from Northern Arizona University, I was elected to the Navajo Nation Council to represent my community, and I’ve been in public service ever since. I subsequently served as a county official and as Vice President of the Navajo Nation before being elected President of the Navajo Nation in 2019.
I’m running to deliver generational change, because we owe it to our children and grandchildren to leave them a future better than the one we inherited. Unfortunately, our current representative isn’t interested in improving the lives of his constituents. His votes to cut healthcare and food assistance from our most vulnerable populations might go over well in the fancy suburb where Crane lives, but out here in rural Arizona, that dog won’t hunt.
[**I’m ready to show up in Congress as a champion for rural Arizona, but I need your support. Can you please split a contribution between my campaign and Sarah McBride today?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250722-1?refcode=em20250722-2203&refcode2=2203_21716_8eQNia&akid=2203%2E21716%2E8eQNia)
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250722-1?refcode=em20250722-2203&refcode2=2203_21716_8eQNia&akid=2203%2E21716%2E8eQNia)**
Our district faces real challenges: from the rising costs of gas, food, childcare, and housing to a lack of accountability in Washington. We need a representative in Congress who’s focused on the issues here in Arizona — not the partisan squabbles of DC. Someone who will put working Arizonans first, create good jobs, lower everyday costs, support our veterans, secure Arizona’s water future, and protect the right to choose.
As the former President of Navajo Nation, I’m ready to deliver on the issues that matter most to our communities, but I can’t get there without your help. [**Please pitch in any amount today, split between my campaign and Sarah McBride.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250722-1?refcode=em20250722-2203&refcode2=2203_21716_8eQNia&akid=2203%2E21716%2E8eQNia)
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250722-1?refcode=em20250722-2203&refcode2=2203_21716_8eQNia&akid=2203%2E21716%2E8eQNia)**
Thank you so much for your support,
Jonathan Nez
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-footer?refcode=em20250722-1&t=1&refcode2=2203_21716_8eQNia&akid=2203%2E21716%2E8eQNia)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/receive_fewer_emails/?t=2&akid=2203%2E21716%2E8eQNia)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/update_your_information/?t=3&akid=2203%2E21716%2E8eQNia)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=2203%2E21716%2E8eQNia)

Email us: [info@sarahmcbride.com](mailto:info@sarahmcbride.com)
Paid for by McBride for Delaware

name: Adam Schwarze, former Navy SEAL
subject: A Navy SEAL vs. Tim Walz’s Radical Lieutenant
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
I’m running for the Senate against a politician who, while our streets burned, pushed lawsuits against law enforcement officials |

---

Fellow Conservative,

My name is Adam Schwarze, and I’m a former Navy SEAL, Marine Corps veteran, and proud Minnesotan **running for U.S. Senate against Tim Walz’s handpicked lieutenant governor—a politician even more radical than her boss.** I’m requesting your support in this critical battle to restore common-sense conservative values to Minnesota.

I hold a Master’s from Harvard, a Master’s Certificate from Johns Hopkins in Security and Nuclear Deterrence, and an MBA. But my proudest achievement is serving our country to make my parents, especially my mom, proud. I’m a regular guy from a working-class family who grew up playing hockey and answered our nation’s call on my 18th birthday in October 2001, just weeks after the 9/11 attacks.** I joined the Marine Corps to defend our country, and as a Navy SEAL, I deployed nine times to over 70 countries, risking my life to protect our freedoms and way of life. Minnesota is at a crossroads. Under Governor Walz and his lieutenant governor, our state has been dragged left by radical policies that fueled the violent unrest in Minneapolis in 2020, **defunded our police** , and burdened hardworking families with higher taxes and inflation. **While our streets burned, the lieutenant governor pushed lawsuits against law enforcement, undermining the officers keeping us safe.** This isn’t the Minnesota I grew up in—or the one any Minnesotan deserves.

clean_body: I’m running for the Senate against a politician who, while our streets burned, pushed lawsuits against law enforcement officials | Fellow Conservative, My name is Adam Schwarze, and I’m a former Navy SEAL, Marine Corps veteran, and proud Minnesotan running for U.S. Senate against Tim Walz’s handpicked lieutenant governor—a politician even more radical than her boss. I’m requesting your support in this critical battle to restore common-sense conservative values to Minnesota. I hold a Master’s from Harvard, a Master’s Certificate from Johns Hopkins in Security and Nuclear Deterrence, and an MBA. But my proudest achievement is serving our country to make my parents, especially my mom, proud. I want to serve you in the U.S. Senate, fighting for the values that have made Minnesota and America great for generations. I’m starting this race with absolutely nothing , but because of the enormity of the race's outcome, I figured there is no harm in asking for your help. At the end of the day, my request of you will end in one of two ways: 1.) . 2.) you read this email without contributing, but you at least now know who I am. I’m not a career politician. I’m a regular guy from a working-class family who grew up playing hockey and answered our nation’s call on my 18th birthday in October 2001, just weeks after the 9/11 attacks. I joined the Marine Corps to defend our country, and as a Navy SEAL, I deployed nine times to over 70 countries, risking my life to protect our freedoms and way of life. Unlike Tim Walz, who retired from the National Guard before his unit deployed to Iraq, I never backed down from serving our nation during war. Minnesota is at a crossroads. Under Governor Walz and his lieutenant governor, our state has been dragged left by radical policies that fueled the violent unrest in Minneapolis in 2020, defunded our police , and burdened hardworking families with higher taxes and inflation. While our streets burned, the lieutenant governor pushed lawsuits against law enforcement, undermining the officers keeping us safe. This isn’t the Minnesota I grew up in—or the one any Minnesotan deserves. If you are reading this email right now and you don’t live in Minnesota, then I’m sure you’ll agree with me that every American must be represented by individuals in Congress who want to build a better version of the America we live in today—individuals of integrity. That’s not who my opponent is. We have an incredible opportunity to elect a Republican to the U.S. Senate next year - something voters in this state haven’t done since they re-elected former Senator Norm Coleman in 2002. As a Republican, I know the odds are tough in a state that hasn’t voted red in a presidential election since 1976. But as a former Navy SEAL, I thrive in tough fights. I’ve faced worse odds overseas and am ready to bring that same grit to Washington. Here’s what I stand for: Restoring law and order by supporting our police and cracking down on crime. Cutting taxes and regulations to ease the burden on retirees and small businesses. Securing our borders to protect our communities and sovereignty. Defending our Second Amendment rights and traditional values. Stopping the radical left’s agenda that’s eroding the America we love. You don’t know me, but you know what men like me stand for and our reputation. So, . This race won’t be easy. The Democrats have deep pockets, and the radical left will stop at nothing to keep this seat. I’m running to serve you, to protect our way of life, and to ensure Minnesota remains a place where faith, family, and freedom thrive. I hope you’ll invest in me right now – any amount will help. With gratitude, Adam Schwarze Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA** | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate

Voter Action Project[https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=25.00&contactdata=0F7ODwR4qA3qfzKnWapCQDS5Jl4ULcTGsVd7tHxCjVDVuoLXnunHgH2m9lfN63+Yf/cAfxncoJkD70XZuPRM5b9rZW8zsNuDxZ29nhSjdhI=]
Atlanta Voice: Georgia seeks another Supreme Court showdown over Voting Rights Act [https://secure.everyaction.com/Z31SNww9Q0CZWIdx46cuSQ2?am=25.00&contactdata=0F7ODwR4qA3qfzKnWapCQDS5Jl4ULcTGsVd7tHxCjVDVuoLXnunHgH2m9lfN63+Yf/cAfxncoJkD70XZuPRM5b9rZW8zsNuDxZ29nhSjdhI=]
Bad news for everyone, Team: The state of Georgia is seeking another Supreme Court showdown over the Voting Rights Act, asking a federal appeals court to interpret the 1965 law in a way that could make it much harder to prove minority votes have been illegally diluted.
Georgia is trying to persuade the judges to make up a new, harder-to-prove standard that could hobble Voting Rights Act lawsuits, less than two years after the U.S. Supreme Court turned back a separate challenge from Alabama to the VRA.
From the AP: " A lawyer for the state asked a three-judge panel of the 11th Circuit U.S. Court of Appeals in Atlanta to overturn a lower court that required lawmakers to draw more Black-majority electoral districts. Georgia’s Republican Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger argued in court filings that the Voting Rights Act is being misused to bolster Democratic election chances and that white voters prefer Republicans for nonracial reasons. "
Opponents of voting rights are hell-bent on dismantling the Voting Rights Act piece by piece and we can't let them. We have to fight in Georgia, Alabama, and beyond to protect our fundamental rights
Pitch in $25 or whatever you can today to give us the resources we need to defend our right to vote in the states, in the courts, and in Congress >> [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2]

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.

name: Wes Moore
subject: We’re in this fight together
body:
It’s no easy battle, but we’re doing everything we can to fight for Maryland.
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=344%2E1086043%2Es1RGLR)
Our communities are being divided, Peter.
The federal government is doing everything in its power to sever its relationship with the states and with our people. But in Maryland, we’re going to keep moving forward mission-first, people always.
**[If you’re with me, chip in to support our movement today.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250722-1?refcode=em-20250722-1&refcode2=344_1086043_s1RGLR&akid=344%2E1086043%2Es1RGLR)**
**Here’s what we’re doing so far:**
📱 We launched the One Application to make it easier for Marylanders to access benefits like Medicaid and SNAP.
🌳 I signed an executive order to make sure we can better track and tackle environmental injustices in our communities so that everyone can drink clean water and breathe cleaner air no matter where they live.
🤝 We partnered with local elected officials, local law enforcement, state’s attorneys, AND community leaders to understand what you all need to make our communities safe.
Navigating these times is a huge undertaking, Peter. We’re constantly updating our priorities to make sure that Maryland gets what’s needed.
We need your help to fuel this work. **[By chipping in $5 or $10 to emails like these, you are helping my team and I do what’s necessary to fight back in the battle against the federal government. Will you pitch in today?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250722-1?refcode=em-20250722-1&refcode2=344_1086043_s1RGLR&akid=344%2E1086043%2Es1RGLR)**

Thanks for all that you do.
Elevate,
Wes

name: Lizzie Fletcher
subject: Make your voice heard for fair maps in Texas!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS
body:
ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/Non-Re-Elect%20Logo.png]
Thanks to everyone who joined our town hall last night to discuss the Texas Legislature’s special session, which began yesterday. We were lucky to hear from the Vice Chair of the House Redistricting Committee, Houston-area Democratic Rep. Jon Rosenthal and former State Senator and current Harris County Commissioner Rodney Ellis.
We discussed three things every Texan can do right now to make sure Governor Abbott and Republican representatives hear our message: Stop the Trump Takeover.
1. Call your Republican Texas Reps and tell them you don’t want them to surrender to Trump and re-redistrict Texas. You can find information about what’s going on and find your rep’s contact information easily thanks to the Texas House Democratic Campaign Committee at www.stopthetexassteal.com [http://www.stopthetexassteal.com] :
http://www.stopthetexassteal.com [http://www.stopthetexassteal.com]
2. Testify before the Texas House Redistricting Committee, which announced three public hearings yesterday. We learned last night that we probably won’t even see the proposed maps before these hearings, making them little more than a performance designed to check a box—not actually listen to Texans. But we’re not going to let them get away with that. This is our chance to show up, speak out, and demand fair maps.
Here are the upcoming public hearings:
🗓️ Thursday, July 24 – Austin
📍 Texas Capitol, Appropriations Room
🗓️ Saturday, July 26 – Houston
📍 University of Houston
🕚 11:00 AM
🗓️ Tuesday, July 29 – Arlington
📍 UT Arlington
🕔 5:00 PM
3. Rally in protest. Events are taking place all over the state. In Harris County, a huge coalition will gather on Saturday before the legislative hearing. Show Texas Republicans that we don’t want them surrendering to Donald Trump and national Republicans.
https://www.mobilize.us/txaflcio/event/817184/ [https://www.mobilize.us/txaflcio/event/817184/]
There is one more thing you can do: Forward this email to friends and family across Texas and urge them to take part. This process is moving quickly. The special session is just 30 days. And we heard last night that the redistricting may move much faster.
This is how we make our voices heard. This is how we fight back. And now is the time.
Together, we can do anything,
Lizzie Signature [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie_signature-1.png]

DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpfe250722?refcode=lpfe250722]

VOLUNTEER [https://secure.ngpvan.com/Ln8e8kjn2Euuh3daVlmozQ2]
3262 Westheimer, PMB 636, Houston, TX 77098
PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS

name: Tim Kaine
subject: re: Tactics I'm using in the Senate against Trump
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]
When Trump won the White House and the GOP secured a trifecta of power in Washington last November, one of the first things I did was ask my Senate staff to put together a list of all the procedural options I would have to counter a President I knew would be determined to push the bounds of his power.
They came up with a motley list of creative options, and in the past six months I’ve used pretty much every single one.
The challenging times we’re facing demand that we discover new ways to resist — and use them as often and as effectively as we can.
Expressing concerns is easy. But that’s often not enough when it comes to Donald Trump and his loyal followers.
If we have the power to stop something, we need to use it — even if that means taking a hard vote, or pushing our colleagues. If you agree, will you add your name to let me know? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/bpXYZmVeUE6_jLcCoca5_A2]
This year, I’ve triggered Senate votes on five privileged resolutions — meaning resolutions that the Senate is required to hold a vote on within a certain period of time. I’ve forced votes on repealing Trump’s fake “national energy emergency,” preventing Trump from taking further military action against Iran, and blocking Trump’s deeply unpopular global tariffs.
We haven’t won on everything — or even most things. But in April, we successfully voted to block President Trump’s ridiculous taxes on Canadian imports.
And even when we lose, these votes can serve a real purpose: They force every Senator to go on the record about Trump’s agenda. And they allow me — a Senator from the minority party — to control the Senate floor, taking up time that might otherwise be used to push through harmful legislation.
Introducing privileged resolutions isn’t a commonly-used tactic. But Donald Trump doesn’t employ commonly-used tactics either! Trump uses whatever tools and powers he can get his hands on to get the things done that he wants to get done — Democrats need to do the same.
I’m going to keep pushing in every way I can to combat this President’s dangerous agenda and protect the interests of Virginians and Americans — and I’m so glad to have you on this team as we continue to navigate these uniquely trying times.
Foundational values that we have taken for granted in the past — the rule of law, our democratic ideals — are being tested with this President and his acolytes in Congress and the Administration.
But we can and must continue to fight for what we believe in.
If you’re with me, I hope you’ll add your name today. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/bpXYZmVeUE6_jLcCoca5_A2]
Thanks for reading,
Tim Kaine
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]
Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
1490-5A Quarterpath Road #272
Williamsburg, VA 23185
Emails like this one are a critical part of staying in touch during this important election year. We value your support, and are so glad to have you on the team. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/P5FfEbyeIEejNQSsVSzanQ2?unsubscribedata=UjUXvYUwrIt5DPJDLcmrRz%2BTTmvsl5coC8kqYEH1RAT86RVymDew03QBqXJA6%2BbkofRdf%2FUfpKgx6%2F9PvC1IPYbYNWwDMPZZt4PxxsVQlqGol0Jxc1Ht3uhBCTko%2B2UW4A4xQ95VQ%2Fiz2Xi4ApuDGSHyGEhgM9N6C9P2nv9EuQSQpNEW5%2B4NCGWnvTIiob%2BxXVR085fUsstEUTIQJTgQLJ7c8LhA5HlKVFsYHkf4Y1ZwBcSyTsELppkHBssUViiRxRtrN9vdwkUi1G5p9xlFBwIGFk7AddeRLTGFntxD0tu%2FnkxexK8w0Zr0wAICSghs1u0D3yv2o13aQ5mX66%2Fl4V8UC9hjOz%2FagRARyhGjqGCV28f6ljb0TPCbTSGffJpmbX2CLWmiher3dLsHVRoA%2BrdiWvUvURKhDfBCuHVZwU8%3D .

subject: Sign the petition now to protect Social Security from cuts!!! >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Democrat Coalition Action Fund
body:
We need your voice now!

| Hi team,

**We wanted to follow up because the GOP's efforts to weaken Social Security are not slowing down, and we can’t afford to stay silent.**

Donald Trump promised not to touch Social Security.

But once in office, he launched one of the most aggressive rollbacks in the program’s history — **slashing staff, closing offices, and making it harder for seniors and people with disabilities to access the benefits they’ve earned.**

This is a direct attack on some of the most vulnerable members of our communities. _That’s why we need your voice now._

**[Will you stand with us and sign the petition to defend Social Security before our deadline at midnight?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111923667/558835011/840040074?refcode=NDCAF_20250722_EMPET_E1_T0_AC_NS_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNDQ0M2ZiYi0xMDY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dZgBapCeqeFV2AbSIC9ijh_CoCXj4fNzF56tGu2VviI=&emci=91a64cfd-a666-f011-8f7c-6045bded8cca&emdi=b4443fbb-1067-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=48524772)** | [ADD YOUR NAME TO DEFEND SOCIAL SECURITY »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111923668/558835012/840040074?refcode=NDCAF_20250722_EMPET_E1_T0_AC_NS_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNDQ0M2ZiYi0xMDY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dZgBapCeqeFV2AbSIC9ijh_CoCXj4fNzF56tGu2VviI=&emci=91a64cfd-a666-f011-8f7c-6045bded8cca&emdi=b4443fbb-1067-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=48524772)

– The New Dems Team

\---------------------------------**
**When Trump was running for office, he vowed to not touch Social Security.**
**Clearly,_he lied:_**

**Since elected, Trump’s administration has:**

\- Cut the Social Security Administration’s staffing by 7,000 jobs – 12% of the workforce
\- Closed regional offices, cutting off access to rural and disabled Seniors
\- Created longer wait times for recipients to receive their benefits

**This is a de facto cut to a program millions of Americans rely on for financial security.**

_We need to fight back now_ – when it’s still possible for us to reverse course and prevent further harm to Social Security and Americans who rely on it for financial security.

clean_body: We need your voice now! | Hi team, We wanted to follow up because the GOP's efforts to weaken Social Security are not slowing down, and we can’t afford to stay silent. Donald Trump promised not to touch Social Security. But once in office, he launched one of the most aggressive rollbacks in the program’s history — slashing staff, closing offices, and making it harder for seniors and people with disabilities to access the benefits they’ve earned. This is a direct attack on some of the most vulnerable members of our communities. That’s why we need your voice now. | Thanks for being part of this fight, – The New Dems Team FWD: \--------------------------------- When Trump was running for office, he vowed to not touch Social Security. Clearly,he lied: Since elected, Trump’s administration has: \- Cut the Social Security Administration’s staffing by 7,000 jobs – 12% of the workforce \- Closed regional offices, cutting off access to rural and disabled Seniors \- Created longer wait times for recipients to receive their benefits This is a de facto cut to a program millions of Americans rely on for financial security. We need to fight back now – when it’s still possible for us to reverse course and prevent further harm to Social Security and Americans who rely on it for financial security. Thank you, New Dems | | Paid for by the New Democrat Coalition Action Fund www.newdemactionfund.com Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 910 17th St NW Suite 1050, Washington, DC 20006 In 2022, NewDems flipped more competitive seats from red-to-blue than any other ideological caucus. name: Desantis...?
subject: “Zero environmental impact”?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Josh Weil, Democrat for US Senate
body:
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/387153444E713877456653376A556574376F314841345859327A4D665362374C42586753794B64635252546F6551413647436E7356536243345157355952664A/editor_images/Screenshot%202025-06-18%20at%2011.41.25%E2%80%AFAM.png]
Friend, DeSantis just claimed the new Alligator Alcatraz detention center has “zero environmental impact.”
Let’s get real. This facility was built on a remote strip in the Everglades, a place that’s supposed to be protected. It’s entire purpose was to abuse the environment to squeeze fear out of “alligator guards” and “python patrol.”
$450 million locked away from oversight. And now they’re trying to spin it like it’s harmless?
I’m tired of the lies.
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/424A3945697637723954694F53666F6A36637A7A37772B417165336974652F793378314E7A316D5846694C2F4A467359655767792F345A6F57617954424D6765/editor_images/Ron%20Desantis%20Florida%20GIF%20by%20GIPHY%20News.gif]
We deserve leadership that deals in truth, not propaganda . We deserve someone who sees people, not profit.
My run for U.S. Senate is doing this differently. This campaign is holding those in power accountable, and aims to implement policy that benefits YOU, not another MAGA obsessed career politician. If that’s something you believe in, will you help fuel it today ?
Pitch in $15 or whatever you can to stop the lies, and bring accountability back!! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/coreemail_jw_kl]

subject: why Republicans are hitting Jon so hard, so early.

body:
#

_One $5 donation, Peter._
That is what we are asking you to make today, to help Jon Ossoff build a winning campaign. We'll explain more shortly, but if you're willing to make that $5 donation right now, please use this link.
[**https://act.electjon.com/go/WIN**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-july25.vLE_dc&refcode=em250722-1-subs&t=1&akid=569%2E5691347%2EjdzUQ5)
Ok here's the deal.
We're looking for a huge number of donations in the next few days. This is important.
As you read this, Jon Ossoff is getting absolutely hammered by attack ads in Georgia. Millions of dollars on TV and online already. It's ridiculous.
It also means we have to ramp up our fundraising earlier than we wish — and with July and August traditionally some of the worst months for grassroots fundraising, the timing is not great.
Jon Ossoff is the only Democrat running for re-election to the Senate in a state won by Trump. That is why Republicans are hitting him so hard, so early in the campaign.
So if Jon is going to defend this seat, we really need to keep hitting our fundraising goals, and donations to emails like this are a huge part of how it happens.
_One $5 donation, Peter. That's what we're asking for before this deadline ends. Can you make it?_
[**Please contribute $5 to Senator Jon Ossoff's re-election to help us get back on track to hit our July fundraising goals. We're being hammered with ads and need to build the war chest it takes to win.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-july25.vLE_dc&refcode=em250722-1-subs&t=2&akid=569%2E5691347%2EjdzUQ5)

[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..5XHj_ZHOCghg_fIUsS03tHk3qcnE9xUki1swqfDCuVo?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=168754726)
Brandolini's Law, named after Italian IT professional Alberto Brandolini, states, "_The amount of energy needed to refute bullsh*t is an order of magnitude bigger than that needed to produce it_."
The amount of bullsh*t in a recent Wall Street Journal OpEd by known propagandist Sadanand Dhume is truly breathtaking. At a mere 754 words, his OpEd spews enough bullsh*t to fill an Olympic stadium. While his rantings would normally fail to meet the standard of a high school civics class, unfortunately, the Wall Street Journal decided it deserved a column that may reach millions of readers. Thus, it demands rebuke and rebuttal. I launched Let's Address This as a human rights project to provide objective, clear, and consistent insights on the critical human rights issues impacting us all. And to debunk bullsh*t. Dhume's history of anti-Muslim and Islamophobic bigotry is well documented, including his open advocacy for ethnic cleansing of Palestinians--which is a war crime. As a human rights lawyer, I am guided by facts, the law, and an ethical duty towards truth. All of which Dhume clearly lacks.
Below I debunk each absurd claim he makes, with receipts, and with the knowledge that Dhume will neither care, nor be capable, of refuting them. Nonetheless, it is our duty to speak truth, especially as corporate media capitulates to, and further enables, fascism. **Let 's Address This.**
**Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..5XHj_ZHOCghg_fIUsS03tHk3qcnE9xUki1swqfDCuVo?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=168754726)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef08b21d-ba10-41de-8397-891edf7f074f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Zohran Mamdani in New York, July 2. Photo: angela weiss/Agence France-Presse/Getty Images
## Preliminary Note
I'll begin by noting that despite the Wall Street Journal's capitulation to Trump, Trump is still currently suing them for defamation. This demonstrates yet again that fascists cannot be appeased, and the WSJ's strategy to promote fascistic ideology does nothing but strengthen Trump. In any case, I refute Dhume's OpEd with a simple format. I cite his sentence verbatim in bold, provide my rebuttal, then repeat by debunking his next absurd claim. Let's dive in.
## Debunking Bullsh*t
Dhume wastes no time spewing bigotry and disinformation with the following title to his OpEd:
> **Mamdani and the Left 's Alliance With Radical Islam: He won't impose Shariah on New York, but his views give plenty of reason for legitimate concern.**
Leading with "radical Islam" and "Shariah" is a classic tactic of shock jock right wing provocateurs who don't have actual arguments, but want to make white conservatives feel afraid. Dhume appears to be among the class of south Asians who believes his proximity _to_ white supremacy will afford him protection _from_ white supremacy. Little does he realize that the same right wing conservatives who take the fear mongering bait on "radical Islam" and "Shariah" are unable to distinguish him (a non-Muslim) from me (a Muslim) in a crowd. Dhume begins his propaganda OpEd as follows.
> **Is Zohran Mamdani a radical Islamist? Contrary to what some Republicans have suggested, the answer is clearly no.**
Dhume never defines "radical Islamist" or what distinguishes that from a "moderate Islamist" or even what an "Islamist" is. Spoiler: It's a made up term to stoke fear and hatred of Muslims or anyone perceived to be Muslim. But Dhume does something even more malicious--after leading with an overtly bigoted question that has no real purpose other than to invoke fear, he positions himself as the reasonable person by saying "clearly no, of course Mamdani is not this big scary thing I just insinuated he is." And the next sentences confirm his vain attempt to mask his own prejudice.
> **But while the democratic socialist front-runner to be New York 's next mayor isn't going to establish a caliphate on the Hudson, we shouldn't be dismissive of Mr. Mamdani's unhealthy obsession with Israel. It is an indication of how radical Islam is gaining acceptance on the left.**
Again, another hot button word "caliphate" to stoke fear. Followed by an accusation of an "unhealthy obsession with Israel." The irony is truly breathtaking. We don't have the luxury of silence. We must fight disinformation with clarity and conviction. Together, we can hold the line--and push it forward. Because someone needs to say what needs to be said, while citing the receipts to back it up.
I'm up to that challenge, and I know with your support, we can make a difference.

clean_body: The WSJ just published an OpEd by a man who is on record supporting the ethnic cleansing of Palestinians--reminding us once more why trust in corporate media has collapsed | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Thank you for being an awesome subscriber! When possible, I hope you'll consider upgrading to a $6/mo or $50/year subscription! The WSJ just published an OpEd by a man who is on record supporting the ethnic cleansing of Palestinians--reminding us once more why trust in corporate media has collapsed | | | Jul 22 | | | | | | | | | | | | Brandolini's Law, named after Italian IT professional Alberto Brandolini, states, "The amount of energy needed to refute bullsht is an order of magnitude bigger than that needed to produce it." The amount of bullsht in a recent Wall Street Journal OpEd by known propagandist Sadanand Dhume is truly breathtaking. At a mere 754 words, his OpEd spews enough bullsht to fill an Olympic stadium. While his rantings would normally fail to meet the standard of a high school civics class, unfortunately, the Wall Street Journal decided it deserved a column that may reach millions of readers. Thus, it demands rebuke and rebuttal. I launched Let's Address This as a human rights project to provide objective, clear, and consistent insights on the critical human rights issues impacting us all. And to debunk bullsht. Dhume's history of anti-Muslim and Islamophobic bigotry is well documented, including his open advocacy for ethnic cleansing of Palestinians--which is a war crime. As a human rights lawyer, I am guided by facts, the law, and an ethical duty towards truth. All of which Dhume clearly lacks. Below I debunk each absurd claim he makes, with receipts, and with the knowledge that Dhume will neither care, nor be capable, of refuting them. Nonetheless, it is our duty to speak truth, especially as corporate media capitulates to, and further enables, fascism. Let 's Address This. Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. | | ---|---|--- Zohran Mamdani in New York, July 2. Photo: angela weiss/Agence France-Presse/Getty Images Preliminary Note I'll begin by noting that despite the Wall Street Journal's capitulation to Trump, Trump is still currently suing them for defamation. This demonstrates yet again that fascists cannot be appeased, and the WSJ's strategy to promote fascistic ideology does nothing but strengthen Trump. In any case, I refute Dhume's OpEd with a simple format. I cite his sentence verbatim in bold, provide my rebuttal, then repeat by debunking his next absurd claim. Let's dive in. Debunking Bullsht Dhume wastes no time spewing bigotry and disinformation with the following title to his OpEd: Mamdani and the Left 's Alliance With Radical Islam: He won't impose Shariah on New York, but his views give plenty of reason for legitimate concern. Leading with "radical Islam" and "Shariah" is a classic tactic of shock jock right wing provocateurs who don't have actual arguments, but want to make white conservatives feel afraid. Dhume appears to be among the class of south Asians who believes his proximity to white supremacy will afford him protection from white supremacy. Little does he realize that the same right wing conservatives who take the fear mongering bait on "radical Islam" and "Shariah" are unable to distinguish him (a non-Muslim) from me (a Muslim) in a crowd. Dhume begins his propaganda OpEd as follows. Is Zohran Mamdani a radical Islamist? Contrary to what some Republicans have suggested, the answer is clearly no. Dhume never defines "radical Islamist" or what distinguishes that from a "moderate Islamist" or even what an "Islamist" is. Spoiler: It's a made up term to stoke fear and hatred of Muslims or anyone perceived to be Muslim. But Dhume does something even more malicious--after leading with an overtly bigoted question that has no real purpose other than to invoke fear, he positions himself as the reasonable person by saying "clearly no, of course Mamdani is not this big scary thing I just insinuated he is." And the next sentences confirm his vain attempt to mask his own prejudice. But while the democratic socialist front-runner to be New York 's next mayor isn't going to establish a caliphate on the Hudson, we shouldn't be dismissive of Mr. Mamdani's unhealthy obsession with Israel. It is an indication of how radical Islam is gaining acceptance on the left. Again, another hot button word "caliphate" to stoke fear. Followed by an accusation of an "unhealthy obsession with Israel." The irony is truly breathtaking. Mamdani was criticized for saying he had no desire as Mayor of New York City to visit Israel, while every other candidate fell over each other to scream their loyalty to Israel--yet now somehow Mamdani is the one "obsessed?" If you 're finding value in this analysis, join us as a free or paid subscriber. And the trifecta of stupidity in Dhume's paragraph ends with an meritless assertion that Mamdani's made up obsession with Israel is now an indictment of the entire left with "radical Islam," which Dhume never defines throughout his piece. He simply makes the blatant statement as fact, and hopes people believe him just because. This isn't journalism. It's hate mongering. Dhume then oscillates back to framing himself as the "good guy" in this dialogue. Over the past few weeks, Mr. Mamdani --a Uganda-born Shiite Muslim of Indian origin--has faced a barrage of attacks and insinuations centered on his Muslim background. This is a correct observation from Dhume, yet it's masked under his apparent lack of self awareness that he himself is part of the barrage of attacks and insinuations. He's literally the bigot making these attacks, while he points fingers at the following bigots. Silicon Valley investor Shaun Maguire accused the candidate of coming from "a culture that lies about everything" and of advancing an "Islamist agenda." Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene of Georgia posted on social media an image of the Statue of Liberty in a black burqa. Conspiracy theorist Laura Loomer claimed that Mr. Mamdani deliberately chose to become a U.S. citizen in 2018 on the anniversary of Hezbollah's release of a 1985 manifesto calling for the destruction of Israel. Again, Dhume should add himself to this list of overt bigots attacking Mamdani for his faith and identity while refusing to grapple with his actual policies. By now I have to believe that Dhume is being deliberately malicious, because I cannot conceive someone is this utterly incompetent in his lack of self awareness. Dhume continues. These contretemps make little sense. Nowhere has the candidate hinted at imposing Shariah, or Islamic law. Mr. Mamdani 's wife, a Syrian-American artist, doesn't wear the hijab. He wants to make New York an "LGBTQIA+ sanctuary city" and once tweeted: "Queer liberation means defund the police." It's hard to think of an idea less likely to resonate with the Muslim Brotherhood or the mullahs in Iran. Yes, Dhume, these attacks absolutely make little sense. So why are you repeating them? Dhume never answers, because doing so would undermine his fear mongering narrative. This is a tactic Dhume employs throughout his article--make a propaganda claim, refuse to back it up or address it because doing so would undermine his entire piece, then position him as the good guy while in reality perpetuating the bigotry. Absurd attacks on Mr. Mamdani enable him to shift focus from statements and actions by him and his family that New Yorkers have legitimate reason to worry about. Finally, nearly half way through his bigoted OpEd, Dhume alludes to statements and actions by Zohran Mamdani and his family that are of "legitimate reason to worry." But before we dive in, is the standard now that every time someone runs for office they are accountable to everything their family members said? Or does that only apply to brown Muslim immigrant candidates? Again, Dhume doesn't answer. Mr. Mamdani 's father, Mahmood, is a Columbia professor of government who has written of his desire to destigmatize suicide bombings. Dhume flat out lies about Professor Mahmood Mamdani's writing. Forbes interviewed actual scholars about Professor Mamdani's academic research and : "Nowhere in [Mamdani's] writing has he advocated for suicide bombing. Nowhere in his writing has he advocated for violence. He's asking us to take a step back and look at it in terms of its causes, in terms of its impact, in terms of its consequences. [Mahmood] Mamdani analyzes the causes of suicide bombing and does not advocate for it, says Nathan Lean, professor of religious studies at North Carolina State University, who told Forbes he has read the book and assigns it in classes he teaches on Islam. Tazeen Ali, a religious studies professor at Washington University in St. Louis who specializes in Islam in the United States, called Maguire's claims "serious mischaracterizations" of Mamdani's work, adding Mamdani's arguments are "not controversial" in academia and his work is "taught widely in undergraduate courses." You 're half way through this article! What do you think? Join us as a free or paid subscriber. Not content with shamelessly lying about Zohran Mamdani's father, Dhume then attacks Mamdani's mother with the following. The candidate 's mother, filmmaker Mira Nair, reportedly tried to have Gal Gadot disinvited from the Oscars because the actress has "openly and repeatedly expressed her support for Israel's military actions." While Dhume attempts to make it seem like Nair is an angry social justice warrior arguing on Twitter, in reality, Nair joined 21 academy members and at least 75 film makers in condemnation of Gadot's open advocacy for the Israeli military's genocide upon the Palestinian people. This affirmation of genocide is a view shared by Amnesty International, The International Court of Justice, the UN Special rapporteur on Palestine, and Israeli Jewish Holocaust scholar Omer Bartov who : My inescapable conclusion has become that Israel is committing genocide against the Palestinian people. I have been teaching classes on genocide for a quarter of a century. I can recognize one when I see one. That Dhume finds it "a legitimate worry" that Nair exercised her free speech to oppose genocide is not an indictment on Nair or on Mamdani, but on Dhume for attempting to whitewash genocide. Dhume continues. Does Mr. Mamdani agree with his parents? Nothing suggests a difference of opinion. I am confident Zohran Mamdani agrees that we should understand the root causes of suicide bombings as a means to stop them, protect free speech and the First Amendment, and unapologetically oppose genocide. That these are controversial takes for Dhume reaffirms nothing more than the lack of seriousness of his OpEd. Mr. Mamdani 's old tweets show that in his 20s he appeared more preoccupied with civil rights for the likes of Boston Marathon bomber Dzhokhar Tsarnaev and U.S.-born al Qaeda leader Anwar al-Awlaki than with the suffering of their victims. Is this what passes for journalism now? Tone policing what you feel people felt based on how they appeared to you? I looked up what Dhume was referring to, and he quotes verbatim right wing bloggers upset that . Note, Mamdani did not tweet with his own commentary, but literally with the default headline that Slate issues when tweeting the article. And what was that allegedly offensive an unforgivable sin that a then 20-year-old Mamdani tweeted? This--what about this is offensive, I wonder? | | ---|---|--- And if one were to read the article, which Dhume clearly did not bother to do, they would find that the article argues, correctly might I add, that No matter how unsympathetic accused terrorists are, the precedents the government sets for them matter outside the easy context of questioning them. When the law gets bent out of shape for Dzhokhar Tsarnaev, it's easier to bend out of shape for the rest of us. This is exactly right. Due process must apply to all accused people. Period. That's what the Constitution requires. Similarly, Dhume's attacks on Mamdani regarding U.S.-born al Qaeda leader Anwar al-Awlaki are likewise absurd. Regarding Awlaki, Mamdani posted about the importance of due process of law, even in the most difficult of situations, because our Constitutional principles are not a pick and choose buffet. I wonder if Dhume supports the U.S. drone strike that killed Awlaki, even though it was in violation of due process? If so, does Dhume also support the drone strike that killed Awlaki's 16-year-old U.S. citizen son, or the one that killed Awlaki's 8-year-old U.S. citizen daughter--both children who had no say or influence over Awlaki's crimes? Would Dhume be okay with a drone killing Awlaki's nephew and nieces? How about his in-laws? His close neighbor? If this line of questioning seems absurd, it should, yet this is what opens up when people like Dhume condemn the need for due process of law. The point of due process of law is to ensure the government is not arbitrarily deciding who gets to die and who gets to live--but that those who are accused of horrific crimes like Awlaki was, face accountability after due process, while innocent children are not murdered. Again, what about not murdering innocent children is controversial to Dhume? In short, Dhume seems to not understand that due process of law applies to all people in this country, for all alleged crimes. It is literally what separates our Democratic Republic from an autocratic regime. The earlier Slate article simply affirms the fact that if we ignore due process for accused terrorists, then we cede the government unwarranted authority to deny due process in other situations as well. And given how right now even Trump appointed judges are of whom who have committed no crime whatsoever--anyone with common sense can see the terrible resulting implications warned of in that Slate article and tweeted out by Zohran Mamdani. Apparently, due process is a bridge too far for Dhume. More recently, Mr. Mamdani has appeared alongside the Turkish-American podcaster Hasan Piker, who once said that America "deserved 9/11." If Dhume is upset at Mr. Mamdani for appearing next to Hasan Piker for his comments about the three thousand innocent Americans killed on 9/11, wait until Dhume learns about the Wall Street Journal columnist who argued for ethnic cleansing of 2 million innocent Palestinians, which is a war crime. Oh wait, that WSJ columnist is Dhume himself--who affirmed himself as pro-genocide with such grotesque remarks. | | ---|---|--- Help me fight back against genocide apologists. Join as a free or paid subscriber. As a human rights lawyer, let me be clear. This is unhinged and sociopathic. The U.S. Government forced Indigenous people to relocate. That was genocide. The Nazis forced Jews to relocate to concentration and death camps. That was genocide. The Myanmar Government forced the Rohingya to relocate. That was genocide. What Dhume advocated for in his Wall Street Journal column is more genocide. I simply remain astonished at Dhume's lack of self awareness. For him, of all people, to object to Piker's remarks about 3000 innocent people, while himself openly demanding genocide of 2 million innocent Palestinians, speaks to a frightening level of sociopathic behavior. Does Dhume object to Eric Adams cutting a secret deal with 34X convicted felon Donald Trump who may well also be in the Epstein files? Nope. Does Dhume object to Andrew Cuomo serving as counsel for accused war criminal Benjamin Netanyahu even as he commits genocide of the Palestinian people? Nope. Dhume's "red line" is a Twitch streamer with no political office for a quote made six years ago (for which Twitch suspended him by the way). Thus, it's no surprise that at this point in his present column, Dhume gives up any pretense of journalistic integrity and simply lists a laundry list of half truths and fear tactics to further smear Mamdani. Each is more ridiculous than the last. Mr. Mamdani also has a long record of anti-Israel activity. Israel is a country. Countries don't have rights. People have rights. The First Amendment gives citizens and non-citizens the right to criticize whatever nation they want. Mamdani has also criticized his parents' ancestral nation of India, and called Indian Prime Minister Modi a "war criminal." Dhume doesn't bring this up, because doing so risks painting Mamdani as a principled leader who opposes injustice wherever it is, as opposed to Dhume's weak attempt to allege some special targeting of Israel. As a co-founder of Bowdoin College 's Students for Justice in Palestine, he backed boycotting Israeli institutions. Again, free speech is a thing in the United States. Perhaps Dhume didn't get the memo. But so far, all Dhume has proven is that Mamdani exercises his peaceful free speech, and that Dhume is apparently anti-free speech. One of his songs during his short-lived rap career praised the Holy Land Five --men convicted by a U.S. court of providing material support for terrorism by funding Hamas. Again, Dhume shamelessly lies. Extensive academic law review peer review articles have addressed the Holy Land Five. I'll cite just one published in 2013 in the Washington and Lee Law School Journal of Civil Rights and Justice, written by acclaimed international criminal defense lawyer Nancy Hollander, which : This case involved nothing more than the provision of charity. Food, medical supplies, wheel-chairs, and backpacks filled with school supplies--just charity. The Holy Land Foundation and these men were never accused of, never charged with, and never convicted of a single violent act. For Dhume to shamelessly lie on the pages of the Wall Street Journal, and for the WSJ to publish it without a single fact check, speaks to the depravity of both. Mr. Mamdani 's immediate response to the Oct. 7, 2023, terrorist attack didn't mention Hamas and instead accused Israel of "occupation" and "apartheid." Dhume neglects to mention that Mamdani's statement led "I mourn the hundreds of people killed across Israel and Palestine the last 36 hours,"--humanizing all civilians killed. And Mamdani did not "accuse" Israel of occupation--it is a factually accurate statement that since 1967 the Israeli military has occupied the Palestinian West Bank. Mamdani did not "accuse" Israel of apartheid--this is the consensus of international human rights orgs including , an , and . And lest Dhume dismiss all these orgs as "antisemitic," also affirming that Israel is an apartheid state are the former , the former , and globally recognized. Dhume hides this from his readers because these facts are inconvenient to his propaganda. Mr. Mamdani refuses to condemn calls to "globalize the intifada." I have never seen media attack someone for something they didn 't say more than how they've attacked Mamdani for not saying a phrase. Let's be clear--Mamdani has never uttered this phrase and has openly discouraged its use. He has additionally committed to an 800% increase in funding to combat antisemitism in New York City. He has the endorsement of the top elected official in New York City who is Jewish, Brad Lander. He has the endorsement of the top elected Congressman with the largest Jewish constituency in America, and who himself is Jewish, Jerry Nadler. | | ---|---|--- Brad Lander, right, cross-endorsed Zohran Mamdani in the Democratic primary for mayor and attended his primary night party. | Heather Khalifa/AP Yet, Dhume ignores all these facts. Doing so is, in my view, an antisemitic dismissal of how Jewish leaders are addressing Mamdani and demonstrating they trust him. Dhume professes to tell these prominent Jewish leaders that he apparently knows better than they do about antisemitism--an absurd hill to die on. Dhume's arrogance is only surpassed by his persistence to double down on his hate and fear mongering. Last month, Mr. Mamdani attended a comedy show with Mahmoud Khalil, an Algerian-Palestinian activist from Columbia University who the Trump administration has accused of "hateful behavior and rhetoric" that threatened Jewish students. At this point I have to wonder if Dhume intended to write a parody, and is just a really bad satirist? Or perhaps he thinks WSJ readers are as incompetent as him? Not only is it absurd to quote Donald "Nazis are very fine people" Trump on antisemitism, but perhaps Dhume missed the memo that a Federal judge ordered Khalil's release from Trump's gulag because the Federal Government failed to provide any evidence of wrongdoing whatsoever. Dhume cites Trump's accusations against Mamdani, but refuses to cite the fact that the accusation was meritless and dismissed. The Trump regime had 104 days to prove their case against Khalil--and failed miserably. Yet, for Dhume, the mere accusation of antisemitism, made by a known white nationalist who dines with Nazis, is enough. It appears Dhume either does not know, or does not care about our Constitutional guarantee of due process for all people in this country. All this may not make Mr. Mamdani an Islamist, but it does make him appear sympathetic to Islamists. Contrary to Dhume's ongoing statements of propaganda, all of this makes Mamdani actually sympathetic towards upholding the United States Constitution, a document Dhume has clearly never read before. So to compensate for his ignorance (and/or arrogance), Dhume throws out scary words like "Islamist" to appease a conservative base (all while refusing to even define the word). Once, someone like Mr. Mamdani couldn 't have been a member in good standing of the left. But for at least two decades leftist groups and Islamists have formed a de facto alliance based on their common contempt for Israel in particular and the West in general. The French call this phenomenon Islamo-gauchisme. This is a statement best described by a phrase borrowed from a high school English teacher of mine, "oral diarrhea." There's no facts presented, no argument made. Just nonsensical crap formed into a sentence without a shred of logic, and by God it stinks. What alliance? Who are the alleged "Islamists" that Dhume is referring to? Earlier he admits Mamdani is not an Islamist (without ever defining the word). Now he claims that Mamdani is in "good standing with the left" because "the left and Islamists" have an alliance. So is Mamdani an Islamist now, contrary to Dhume's statement earlier that Mamdani is "clearly not" an Islamist? I am astonished that anyone thinks Dhume is a rationally thinking human being worth publishing. And what the hell do the French have to do with a NYC Mayoral election? For Islamic extremists, the alliance with the left provides cover. Someone who seeks to annihilate Israel because he can 't stomach the idea of Jews living on what was once Muslim-controlled land can clothe this atavistic urge in the respectable language of human rights. Again, Dhume alleging some magical alliance exists does not make it so. Such a statement should be offensive to anyone committed to facts or truth. Moreover, the issue is not "Jews living on once Muslim-controlled lands," the issue is Israeli genocide, apartheid, and military occupation--three things affirmed by global and Israeli human rights orgs, but something Dhume refuses to have the integrity or courage to address. Again, admitting to such facts would undermine Dhume's propaganda claims--so he does his best to ignore them altogether. Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. Finally, claiming Mamdani has an "atavistic urge" to "annihilate Israel" is not only defamatory, it speaks to Dhume's ignorance about the history of Jews and Muslims prior to western colonialism and the fall of the Ottoman empire. As I've written in detail , for centuries Muslims and Jews lived peacefully in Jerusalem, with both suffering only at the hands of--not each other--but of Christian Crusaders. Crusaders who violently murdered exiled both Jews and Muslims. Meanwhile, it was under Caliph Omar, then General Saladin, then the Mamulks, and in Almoravid Spain, that Jews lived for centuries in peace under Muslim rule, protected from harm, forced conversion, or property seizure. But the oddness of the left --in theory allergic to God--making allowances for Muslims that it won't make for others hasn't escaped notice. The oddness of Dhume--an avowed atheist--lecturing anyone about being allergic to God is rich. Moreover, the left is not opposed to God, it is opposed to theocracy and the mixing of religion and state. The absurdity of a brown atheist immigrant siding with white supremacist ultra nationalist right wing MAGAs who want to establish a Christian nationalist theocracy is a contradiction so severe that Hollywood would reject it as unrealistic. Yet here is Dhume, the WSJ's walking talking contradiction. " Most leftists . . . have no difficulty fearing and opposing Hindu nationalists, zealous Buddhist monks, and the messianic Zionists of the settler movement," the Princeton philosopher Michael Walzer wrote in 2015. Dhume using this quote from Walzer is both contradictory and confusing. His citing Walzer is contradictory because throughout the piece Dhume ignores Israel's illegal military occupation of Palestine, and deems it an illegitimate reason to be critical of Israel. Yet now, he cites Jewish scholar Michael Walzer to state that people are legitimate to "fear and oppose the Zionist settler movement." So, which is it? Is Mamdani wrong to condemn Israel's illegal occupation, or is Walzer right to condemn Israel's illegal military occupation? Dhume doesn't give an answer because he prefers to twist words to meet his needs, and hopes his readers aren't intelligent enough to notice his contradictions. Dhume then continues quoting Michael Walzer as follows: Yet the left won 't blame religious extremism for "Islamist militants who kidnap schoolgirls, or murder heretics, or tear down the ancient monuments of rival civilizations." This is a lie. The left absolutely blames religious extremism for the mentioned atrocities--but we also don't just stop there. We recognize, as scholarship has repeatedly proven, that actions like the illegal invasion of Iraq, the mass murdering of civilians in Afghanistan, and the perpetual bombing of Palestinians, . This is not a secret. Do Walzer and Dhume assume that people in the Middle East would not be upset that western wars in the 21st century? Why is Mr. Mamdani unwilling to reassure voters by adopting a more moderate stance? The answer to this question could shape the future of New York. Finally, despite making this meritless conclusion, the fact is that Dhume fails to state a single extremist position taken by Zohran Mamdani. Instead, Dhume opts to demonize Mamdani for demanding due process of law in all matters, for agreeing with global and Israeli human rights orgs that the Israeli government is committing genocide, and for apparently not strongly enough condemning a phrase he's never used in the first place. None of Dhume has written amounts to actual journalism, but instead, propaganda dressed up as intellectualism from a man who openly and shamelessly advocates for the genocide of Palestinians. A Final Note I must reaffirm and remind the reader that at no point does Dhume even attempt to address any of Mamdani's actual policies. He ignores Mamdani's plan to increase funding by 800% to combat antisemitism. His plan to support working class New Yorkers by raising taxes by a mere 2% on millionaires, to freeze rent, or to make childcare and busses free. He ignores how Mamdani's policies are proven models. How in 2022, when Massachusetts raised taxes on millionaires by 4%, it resulted in a 40% increase in millionaires, a decrease in poverty, and strengthened the overall Massachusetts economy. Dhume's blatant racist and Islamophobic attacks are an attempt to distract from Mamdani's proven economic policies. Policies that will combat wealth and income inequality, elevate the lives of ordinary New Yorkers, and demonstrate that we do not need to bend the knee to billionaires. That is the real story that Dhume and the WSJ are trying to bury. Do not let them. Conclusion It took me more than 4600 words to debunk Dhume's 754 word OpEd. Meaning, according to Brandolini's Law, it took a ~6X order of magnitude to refute Dhume's bullsht. What Sadanand Dhume published in the Wall Street Journal wasn't journalism--it is dangerous propaganda. It relied on racism, fear-mongering, and deliberate distortion to smear a principled public servant who dares to speak inconvenient truths. Dhume recycled every tired Islamophobic trope and packaged it as reasoned concern. And corporate media once again gave it a national platform--no fact-checks, no pushback, no shame. And it was disseminated to millions of people in print and online. So, this is where I need your help to fight back. I thank you for reading this. Now, I ask you to share this across your spheres of influence. In a time when billionaires and billion dollar corporations control major media, when facts are buried beneath dog whistles, and when fascism marches under the guise of "security," Let 's Address This exists to do what they refuse to do: tell the truth, bring the receipts, name the rot, and demand better. If this work resonates with you--if you believe in truth over fear, in facts over propaganda, and in justice over bigotry--then I invite you to support this work. Share this article. Talk about it. And if you're not yet a subscriber, . We don't have the luxury of silence. We must fight disinformation with clarity and conviction. Together, we can hold the line--and push it forward. Because someone needs to say what needs to be said, while citing the receipts to back it up. I'm up to that challenge, and I know with your support, we can make a difference. If my name and face are familiar, it's not just because I ran for Congress in 2024.

For more than 30 years, I was a TV news anchor, uplifting the stories of Central Pennsylvanians. I flew over New York City with the Pennsylvania National Guard after 9/11, delivered the heartbreaking news that _Roe v. Wade_ had been overturned on live television, and shared the stories of businesses closing, job losses, and families struggling to put food on the table and keep a roof over their heads.

**I am a dedicated truth teller with decades of experience standing up for our neighbors.**

Now I am working to replace former Freedom Caucus Chair and _**MAGA insurrectionist Scott Perry. **_
* He has praised the GOP budget bill that just passed. You know — the one that will kick millions of hard-working Americans off Medicaid, shutter rural hospitals, and give tax cuts to the wealthy 1%.
* He has supported a national abortion ban with no exceptions.
* He helped plan the January 6 insurrection, where Americans lost their lives.

Scott Perry is too extreme for PA-10, and we deserve better.

**Peter: PA-10 is a make-or-break district to flip the U.S. House.** We have a serious shot at winning this district, but my opponent is backed by mountains of dark money and billionaire donors. The support of grassroots donors like you is crucial to our success.

[**Now is when it counts, Peter. It is important that I raise as much money as possible in these first few weeks to show Scott Perry and MAGA Republicans that Pennsylvanians want him OUT. clean_body: | = Hi Peter. I'm Janelle Stelson, the Democrat running to oust Scott Perry in PA-10 — a major tossup district. In 2024, this was the second closest red to blue challenger race in the entire country — we lost by just 5,133 votes. We can flip this district from red to blue this time, but it is going to take every single one of us. = | = If my name and face are familiar, it's not just because I ran for Congress in 2024. For more than 30 years, I was a TV news anchor, uplifting the stories of Central Pennsylvanians. I flew over New York City with the Pennsylvania National Guard after 9/11, delivered the heartbreaking news that Roe v. Wade had been overturned on live television, and shared the stories of businesses closing, job losses, and families struggling to put food on the table and keep a roof over their heads. I am a dedicated truth teller with decades of experience standing up for our neighbors. Now I am working to replace former Freedom Caucus Chair and MAGA insurrectionist Scott Perry. He has praised the GOP budget bill that just passed. You know — the one that will kick millions of hard-working Americans off Medicaid, shutter rural hospitals, and give tax cuts to the wealthy 1%. He has supported a national abortion ban with no exceptions. He helped plan the January 6 insurrection, where Americans lost their lives. Scott Perry is too extreme for PA-10, and we deserve better. Peter: PA-10 is a make-or-break district to flip the U.S. House.** We have a serious shot at winning this district, but my opponent is backed by mountains of dark money and billionaire donors. The support of grassroots donors like you is crucial to our success. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Help us launch this campaign with the momentum we need to oust Scott Perry once and for all. With gratitude, Janelle = Janelle Stelson = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Inbox overcrowded? subject: Johnson shutting down House in order to avoid a vote on releasing Epstein files
body:

# [Demand Mike Johnson stop blocking a vote on releasing the Epstein files](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tac0fec1e-dba0-4913-b6ec-617e97845256/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T2b2cf2c0-1240-4d20-aff2-3d366154d87a/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | An overwhelming majority of Americans want the Epstein files released, and an overwhelming majority of House members -- Democratic and Republican -- would vote for it if given the chance. **But Speaker Mike Johnson refuses to put it up for a vote. Because Donald Trump wants the whole thing to go away. And Johnson is too chicken**** to stand up to him and do the right thing.** He's even gone so far as shutting down the House until September in order to avoid a vote. Johnson's responsibility is to the American people, not a wannabe dictator, or the pedophiles who might be outed if those files are released. Tell him to do his job NOW. [**Add your name: Demand Mike Johnson stop blocking a vote on releasing the Epstein files**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T46fd3629-e9d7-42cf-b71c-69e4d9f8df2b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
