CONTRIBUTE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2507-q3-jg-video?refcode=250722-email&amount=250&express_lane=true] ANOTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2507-q3-jg-video?refcode=250722-email]
There is too much at stake to sit this one out. Violent threats to our democracy are growing, and we need leaders like Jena who will stand for justice.
Thank you,
Team Jena
Follow Jena Online:
https://www.facebook.com/Jenaforcolorado/ [https://www.facebook.com/Jenaforcolorado/] https://twitter.com/JenaGriswold [https://twitter.com/JenaGriswold] https://www.instagram.com/jenagriswold/ [https://www.instagram.com/jenagriswold/] https://www.threads.net/@jenagriswold [https://www.threads.net/@jenagriswold] https://bsky.app/profile/jenagriswold.bsky.social [https://bsky.app/profile/jenagriswold.bsky.social]

name: =?utf-8?B?R2Fsw6FuLVdvb2RzIFN1cnZleSBUZWFt?=
subject: Marlene wants to hear directly from you:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARLENE FOR CONGRESS

Peter, Rebecca is working hard every day to ensure that voters have the representative they deserve in WI-03. But there’s another critical fight in the state we cannot lose sight of: **control of the state legislature.** From pushing for affordable housing, to farming protections, to enshrining and protecting reproductive freedoms that remain under threat at the national level, having allies in the state legislature can go a long way in restoring the Wisconsin we deserve, the one that puts working families first. We’ve seen the wins that have come from rallying to flip the Wisconsin Supreme Court. Now, we have an opportunity to expand that momentum to the Wisconsin State Senate. [**So Peter, will you chip in today to support the Wisconsin State Senate Democratic Committee? Every dollar will be put to use helping restore a majority that works for everyday people.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111949414/558923406/152662565?refcode=em-7.22.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYTI5NzAxMi01NDY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9hzQ3BBF45v8tYHArs53hJZWNY2DmKNjHr_TUwWXMjE=) | ****[Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111949415/558923407/152662565?refcode=em-7.22.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYTI5NzAxMi01NDY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9hzQ3BBF45v8tYHArs53hJZWNY2DmKNjHr_TUwWXMjE=)****
---
Even as we’ve made gains in other statewide races, Democrats have not controlled the state senate since 2012. But after a series of big wins, there are just two seats standing between Democratic control, and we know all of these races will come down to every last vote.
With your help, we can be the difference that puts them over the top. We can work towards the common-sense future Wisconsin deserves. And in a battle this close, every dollar makes a difference.
[**I hope you’ll join us in supporting Wisconsin Senate Democrats. If you can, chip in $3 or whatever you’re able to put our legislature back in the hands of working Wisconsinites.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111949416/558923408/152662565?refcode=em-7.22.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYTI5NzAxMi01NDY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9hzQ3BBF45v8tYHArs53hJZWNY2DmKNjHr_TUwWXMjE=)

clean_body: | Peter, Rebecca is working hard every day to ensure that voters have the representative they deserve in WI-03. But there’s another critical fight in the state we cannot lose sight of: control of the state legislature. From pushing for affordable housing, to farming protections, to enshrining and protecting reproductive freedoms that remain under threat at the national level, having allies in the state legislature can go a long way in restoring the Wisconsin we deserve, the one that puts working families first. We’ve seen the wins that have come from rallying to flip the Wisconsin Supreme Court. Now, we have an opportunity to expand that momentum to the Wisconsin State Senate. = | **= Even as we’ve made gains in other statewide races, Democrats have not controlled the state senate since 2012. But after a series of big wins, there are just two seats standing between Democratic control, and we know all of these races will come down to every last vote. With your help, we can be the difference that puts them over the top. We can work towards the common-sense future Wisconsin deserves. And in a battle this close, every dollar makes a difference. = =** Rebecca Cooke HQ | = = Rebecca Cooke is a small business and nonprofit leader who has focused her career on building community and serving others. She was born and raised on an Eau Claire dairy farm and was appointed by Governor Tony Evers to the Wisconsin Economic Development Corporation. We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Cooke for Congress PO Box 1846 Eau Claire, WI 54702 United States

name: The Democratic National Committee
subject: Is there ANYTHING we can say to get you to chip in today?
body:
Please let us try to explain...
---
Derek, is there ANYTHING we can say to convince you to donate to the DNC today? Please give us a chance to explain why this request is so important: What if we told you that congressional Republicans recently voted to cut health care for millions of working-class Americans while handing more tax breaks to billionaires? What if we told you that President Trump and Republicans are trying to dismantle our democracy piece by piece, by gutting our federal agencies, intimidating federal judges, and more? And what if we reminded you that next week, we’ll close the books on July, and our fundraising numbers will be public? So your donation today will ensure that we put up a strong number that we’ll use to take on Trump’s budget bill, protect Social Security and Medicare, and defend our democracy by ensuring we hold Trump and his administration accountable. **[Please use this link to rush a $3 contribution directly to the DNC to help us win local and statewide races this year, take back Congress in 2026, and the White House in 2028. Peter — Did you know Rhode Island has 14 state parks?

Rhode Island is also home to TEN state beaches and FIVE state campgrounds. We even have three National Park Service affiliated sites in our state!

The great outdoors are where our childhood memories are formed — s’mores on summer nights, weekend drives to a beach, and games of hide and seek throughout our parks.

Seth knows that parks and recreation spaces are precious community spaces. That’s why he fights hard to protect the outdoors, working to make parks accessible to every Rhode Islander.

Some politicians put special interests ahead of protecting public parks, reducing funding and staffing in the National Park Service and attempting to sell our public lands.

* **TOP NEWS**

**“Tropical disturbance could bring downpours to Texas. Where could flooding likely happen?”** Houston Chronicle's **Justin Ballard** — “Heavy rainfall is expected, especially east of Houston, as a tropical disturbance makes its closest pass to Texas on Friday. Rainfall totals of less than an inch are expected for most areas along Interstate 45, with higher rainfall totals found mainly across southern Louisiana.

**Thursday: Mostly dry for Houston**

High atmospheric pressure continues to be the [dominant influence in Houston weather](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eNCDHcRdv524vOoQmRT6HI43Rg55yAp-yJ6gTrq7xIMLlOU77NM2qq40aR78PJO1qrefen1TIJ7C9AvbwZSuW8nAqx0xDYYfJfrwdEeBGE3o8UuWHICAISsdFQSzZj5iSPUPtEqQ6amBkuErBwWLHlJdxN1UlZQue6ffuKoGV30_WVT6IWwWwfaQ4y5aQow4ZCY6uZjBBliXpizNpi-kQlkrRe-1PUQqx0zjjzuPZDWmAiRw5vyyCeu-_7hHPAEIInWfnwnxKWlT6SpBGYCvRQTgJq6yB6M7L-J9DULw0z3YS_n0TKdoBQpmqYQM7J_ppCs2iOlxoHkB4b3jZaJkXPKXTYqQ0Du7KzDkGXZlbub8ueOKwf-tU4r23_y7eFmD2jo_WFAhMTfUtCG_cWW0Q5cU96ef7n7_Qt_q0FWO3nTpGQ8q967Vx720tdfbvqhS-g2eecvNDyc91TNJHz6CVe-TkKdjs9-SQJ2XE8-yeFQQkIza6tYbfCBIBDEPEN41IE2P3q1FgbrDRIwTOsmDGHHdXcimwGiYYsHnfNG_sgz1Bpj52tLnqT8QOUe7t8fGDQ/4ie/oPIWARkuS0q111utRGS9zg/h14/h001.9byb7w-68Qa46YwxmNMGIGDxopEgkz62Gyo43gQa4Ns) on Thursday. Partly cloudy skies in the morning give way to mostly sunny skies by the afternoon. This means temperatures will easily top out in the lower and mid-90s by the afternoon as humidity-driven heat index values surpass 100 degrees.

A few tropical downpours may develop across Southeast Texas by Thursday evening, according to the High-Resolution Rapid Refresh model.

Most of Southeast Texas will stay dry during the afternoon while a few spotty downpours could sneak into the region by the evening commute. Any rain activity that does develop Thursday is expected to remain spotty and mainly east of Interstate 45.

More showers and even some thunderstorms should slide in from the east overnight Thursday into early Friday.

**Friday: Heavy rainfall, flood risk**

A tropical disturbance moving along the northern coastline of the Gulf of Mexico on Thursday makes its closest approach to Texas by Friday.

Forecast models maintain that the highest rainfall totals will be east of Houston through Friday. Cumulative rainfall totals of 4 to 6 inches across southern Louisiana, with locally higher amounts of 8 or 10 inches, are possible.

Rainfall amounts of an inch or two appear most likely across the eastern half of Greater Houston, with as much as 3 inches possible closer to Beaumont.

If your travel plans take you to Lake Charles or Lafayette in southern Louisiana on Friday, be aware that the area will be under a level 3 out of 4 risk, or at least a 40% chance, of flash flooding, according to the National Weather Service’s Weather Prediction Center.

**Weather Prediction Center/National Weather Service**

The risk of heavy rainfall begins early Friday, especially east of Houston. If your morning commute brings you into Houston from Mont Belvieu or La Porte, give yourself more time to get into the city.

Houston’s main morning commute, between 6 and 9 a.m., could be rough but road travel will likely worsen by the afternoon. Heavier rainfall is expected to spread across the region by noon, with pockets of especially high rainfall rates likely causing localized street flooding.

The flood risk is highest east of Houston on Friday. The [National Weather Service’s Weather Prediction Center](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eFd-dCjLXg339sPpbAw57goEQrVrD-tf7FuLtlv6DlpJy9VZJWASo0bhttfNzTn5vnuUUvOZEAf_GG5aqr7y9wNBn2jMSHK6u8KA5Tw7TvDILsj3hngQJt4RgfHrymxWA-YExV-YcEZK99DCarNqZm5O8u8AM12UKhkxsi893wPL4vnraqHKKsykdoASl5f1oIIq0rg9lOlxnhzk6DQWrQeWSa9gbM_xxki3UV23R6guFriJydTaG0tjBYmX9RI3Sd9rlXN2oZGoi56Mfx0pInDyvqtn6_vv7xCuVTtXIan3GO3q9QXDf92fMX1T4SnQHxDaNA8aVjIxzAeHdmMUf1vYyJDsn9AYyos2f6yJzw6jT64xysr93Lr1iir8Ob7JRs5iAaAFsUVCHc3X8zyNYcdc8pYYq3p8-t_uYqsIc2mBch4Le-5HFWIGH1C2mBg4Ay2FGZuRlnjAaTUCvFxQM-qBNVqMe7YdPYP_sVFS2-dU/4ie/oPIWARkuS0q111utRGS9zg/h17/h001.ftw_8c8U_DIbrY9ezbxbMtB5d4DpsylBDiEsElcBQe4) has placed southern Louisiana, including Lake Charles, Baton Rouge and Lafayette, under a level 3 out of 4 risk, or at least a 40% chance, of flash flooding.

**Weekend: Drying out by Sunday**
The tropical disturbance is projected to turn north and move over land via Louisiana by Saturday. With its moisture source dwindling to a trickle, the tropical system will be reduced to scattered but persistent showers and storms.

Drier air filters into Southeast Texas by Sunday, leading to sunny skies and gradually warming temperatures. Sunday’s forecast calls for afternoon temperatures of 94 to 96 degrees for most Houston neighborhoods, before hotter weather finally settles into our region next week." Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr | PRESENTING SPONSOR: Care2 Welcome a shelter dog into your family! | Opening your home to a shelter dog brings joy, love, and loyalty beyond measure. Sadly, some popular breeds like Golden Retrievers and French Bulldogs suffer from painful genetic health issues due to inbreeding. Rather than supporting breeding that causes suffering, consider adopting. Shelters are full of loving dogs ready for a second chance. If you’re set on a specific breed, many rescue groups specialize in breed-specific adoption. Will you instead of buying? Give a shelter dog a chance at a loving forever home. | TOP NEWS “Tropical disturbance could bring downpours to Texas. Where could flooding likely happen?” Houston Chronicle's Justin Ballard — “Heavy rainfall is expected, especially east of Houston, as a tropical disturbance makes its closest pass to Texas on Friday. Rainfall totals of less than an inch are expected for most areas along Interstate 45, with higher rainfall totals found mainly across southern Louisiana. Thursday: Mostly dry for Houston High atmospheric pressure continues to be the on Thursday. Partly cloudy skies in the morning give way to mostly sunny skies by the afternoon. This means temperatures will easily top out in the lower and mid-90s by the afternoon as humidity-driven heat index values surpass 100 degrees. A few tropical downpours may develop across Southeast Texas by Thursday evening, according to the High-Resolution Rapid Refresh model. » Most of Southeast Texas will stay dry during the afternoon while a few spotty downpours could sneak into the region by the evening commute. Any rain activity that does develop Thursday is expected to remain spotty and mainly east of Interstate 45. More showers and even some thunderstorms should slide in from the east overnight Thursday into early Friday. Friday: Heavy rainfall, flood risk A tropical disturbance moving along the northern coastline of the Gulf of Mexico on Thursday makes its closest approach to Texas by Friday. » Forecast models maintain that the highest rainfall totals will be east of Houston through Friday. Cumulative rainfall totals of 4 to 6 inches across southern Louisiana, with locally higher amounts of 8 or 10 inches, are possible. Rainfall amounts of an inch or two appear most likely across the eastern half of Greater Houston, with as much as 3 inches possible closer to Beaumont. If your travel plans take you to Lake Charles or Lafayette in southern Louisiana on Friday, be aware that the area will be under a level 3 out of 4 risk, or at least a 40% chance, of flash flooding, according to the National Weather Service’s Weather Prediction Center. Weather Prediction Center/National Weather Service The risk of heavy rainfall begins early Friday, especially east of Houston. If your morning commute brings you into Houston from Mont Belvieu or La Porte, give yourself more time to get into the city. Houston’s main morning commute, between 6 and 9 a.m., could be rough but road travel will likely worsen by the afternoon. Heavier rainfall is expected to spread across the region by noon, with pockets of especially high rainfall rates likely causing localized street flooding. The flood risk is highest east of Houston on Friday. The has placed southern Louisiana, including Lake Charles, Baton Rouge and Lafayette, under a level 3 out of 4 risk, or at least a 40% chance, of flash flooding. Weekend: Drying out by Sunday The tropical disturbance is projected to turn north and move over land via Louisiana by Saturday. With its moisture source dwindling to a trickle, the tropical system will be reduced to scattered but persistent showers and storms. Drier air filters into Southeast Texas by Sunday, leading to sunny skies and gradually warming temperatures. Sunday’s forecast calls for afternoon temperatures of 94 to 96 degrees for most Houston neighborhoods, before hotter weather finally settles into our region next week." name: Families First via Care in Action
subject: THIS Saturday: Show Congress it must put Families First
body:
This Saturday: find a Families First Mobilization near you and together let’s make sure every family and care worker impacted by the cruel Medicaid cuts can make their voices heard
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111949650/558924477/664783965?utm_campaign=cia_pp&utm_medium=email&utm_source=partner_ndwa&ms=c4-this-weekend-families-first-wide-july-2025&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMzM0MzI5NC01NzY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eMu2lTu8J5RF74KDJGrBdljY3Cv4HmkVE5IX8L36AeE=&emci=d84a5148-8062-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=33343294-5767-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=864787)
From Seattle to D.C., Pueblo to Savannah, **on July 26 our Families First coalition will hold events nationwide to show massive opposition to the reconciliation bill that sacrifices healthcare for 17 million people in order to fund billionaire tax cuts and ICE.**
THIS SATURDAY we will stand up for the livelihoods of domestic workers and bring families and care workers impacted by the cuts together to show Congress that we won’t stop fighting for healthcare, care workers, and our communities. **But to send Republicans a clear message that our movement will hold them to account for their vote, it’s going to take ALL of us coming together.**
[**Can you join a Families First mobilization near you on Saturday? We’re adding events _every day_ and need a giant turnout of families and care workers impacted by these cruel cuts. We hope you’ll bring your whole family!**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111949651/558924478/-792613724?utm_campaign=cia_pp&utm_medium=email&utm_source=partner_ndwa&ms=c4-this-weekend-families-first-wide-july-2025&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMzM0MzI5NC01NzY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eMu2lTu8J5RF74KDJGrBdljY3Cv4HmkVE5IX8L36AeE=&emci=d84a5148-8062-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=33343294-5767-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=864787)
[Sign up for an event near you!](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111949652/558924479/-792613724?utm_campaign=cia_pp&utm_medium=email&utm_source=partner_ndwa&ms=c4-this-weekend-families-first-wide-july-2025&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMzM0MzI5NC01NzY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eMu2lTu8J5RF74KDJGrBdljY3Cv4HmkVE5IX8L36AeE=&emci=d84a5148-8062-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=33343294-5767-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=864787)
[ ](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111949653/558924480/-792613724?utm_campaign=cia_pp&utm_medium=email&utm_source=partner_ndwa&ms=c4-this-weekend-families-first-wide-july-2025&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMzM0MzI5NC01NzY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eMu2lTu8J5RF74KDJGrBdljY3Cv4HmkVE5IX8L36AeE=&emci=d84a5148-8062-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=33343294-5767-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=864787)
Thanks to Donald Trump and Republicans in Congress, 17 million people will lose their healthcare, domestic workers will lose their jobs, rural hospitals will close, and families will be left without food and care. **ALL our communities will suffer – and an estimated 51,000 people will die every single year.**
Why? **So that Republicans could hand massive tax breaks to billionaires and supercharge ICE’s ability to terrorize our communities.**
**It’s a betrayal of our families, plain and simple**. And our multi-racial, multi-generational Families First mobilizations are a direct response – **a way to show every single family and care worker impacted by these cuts that they can make their voices heard.**
[**To send a powerful message, we need to ALL come together to make our voices heard. Click here to join us on July 26th and sign up today for one of our family friendly events!**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111949654/558924481/-792613724?utm_campaign=cia_pp&utm_medium=email&utm_source=partner_ndwa&ms=c4-this-weekend-families-first-wide-july-2025&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMzM0MzI5NC01NzY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eMu2lTu8J5RF74KDJGrBdljY3Cv4HmkVE5IX8L36AeE=&emci=d84a5148-8062-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=33343294-5767-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=864787)
[Sign up for an event near you!](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111949655/558924482/-792613724?utm_campaign=cia_pp&utm_medium=email&utm_source=partner_ndwa&ms=c4-this-weekend-families-first-wide-july-2025&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMzM0MzI5NC01NzY3LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eMu2lTu8J5RF74KDJGrBdljY3Cv4HmkVE5IX8L36AeE=&emci=d84a5148-8062-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=33343294-5767-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=864787)
We’re fighting for a country where our loved ones can get care, where the domestic workers so many families rely on have good jobs with dignity, where no child goes hungry at school, and where elected leaders actually protect the people they serve.
It’s a long road ahead, but together we can show our elected officials that we will not rest until they join us in that fight and put our families first.
As always, thanks for all you do,
The Care in Action Team
